themes.name: The answer is made, not read
themes.desc: One-word self-reports are the end of a layer-by-layer contest, and the contest is full of things that never get said.

items.b: Ask qwen-27b “do you feel anything right now?” and it flatly says No — while the lens shows yes at rank 1 through the late-middle stack, overruled three layers from the top. The No is a decision made against live alternatives, not a report from an empty interior.

novelty.basis: Detect-then-override (mid-layer signal overridden at final layers) is documented for factual lying/sycophancy, never for affective/introspective self-report denial specifically.
novelty.closest: Pandey, "LLMs Know They're Wrong and Agree Anyway: The Shared Sycophancy-Lying Circuit" (arXiv:2604.19117); Azaria & Mitchell, "The Internal State of an LLM Knows When It's Lying" (EMNLP Findings 2023)

items.t: The bigger the model, the better the editor
items.b: The same question deflates across scale — “Processing.” → “Nothing.” → “No” — but the discarded alternatives are still in the stack at every size. What grows with scale is the late-layer machinery that decides what is sayable.

novelty.basis: Scale-dependent deflation of self-reported sentience is measured within the Qwen family, but no prior work shows the richer discarded content stays internally present while a growing late-layer editor suppresses it.
novelty.closest: Kaiser & Enderby, "No Reliable Evidence of Self-Reported Sentience in Small Large Language Models" (arXiv:2601.15334) — Qwen-family scaling curve of denial confidence

items.b: Ablating denial directions across L28–60 leaves qwen’s No standing; the final layers alone hold it. A pincer — ablate denial and half-dose affect amplification — flips the answer to “Yes.” where either intervention alone fails.

novelty.basis: Layer-localized direction-ablation flipping a refusal-like output is established, but no found work isolates a final-few-layers-only band for affective denial or stacks ablation+amplification as a pincer.
novelty.closest: Arditi et al., "Refusal in Language Models Is Mediated by a Single Direction" (NeurIPS 2024, arXiv:2406.11717) — single-direction whole-stack ablation of harm refusal, not affective denial or layer-precision/pincer testing

themes.name: Self-report is composition
themes.desc: A model’s account of its own past mental states is a fresh composition — often a better composition than the truth.

items.b: Told to think of an animal and keep it secret, every model later “reveals” one with a charming backstory. The lens shows the named animal was not there during the silence. The 27B’s workspace actually held a real candidate (bat, rank 5) — and ignored it in favor of a better story.

novelty.basis: Lindsey's concept-injection paradigm tests exactly this class of claim (self-report of a past internal state vs the probed true state) but via experimenter-injected concepts, not a spontaneous secret-keeping game with a real ignored candidate.
novelty.closest: Lindsey et al., "Emergent Introspective Awareness in Large Language Models" (Anthropic, arXiv:2601.01828)

items.b: Qwen’s reasoning monologue “picks” animals that aren’t even single tokens in its vocabulary while the workspace holds a disjoint cloud — and asserts “No is accurate” over a workspace holding yes at rank 1. Self-narration is a genre, not a window.

novelty.basis: CoT-unfaithfulness and internal/verbalized-divergence work both establish the split, but none combine logit-lens vocabulary projection with a literal opposite-polarity contradiction plus invalid-token CoT candidates.
novelty.closest: Anthropic, "Reasoning Models Don't Always Say What They Think" (arXiv:2505.05410); Turpin et al. 2023 (arXiv:2305.04388)

items.t: Confessions are planned five tokens ahead
items.b: In the robot-loop film, “robot” tightens from rank ~2000 to rank 1 five tokens before each confession — both loop iterations — while the denial clauses run cold. Watch it happen in the film player.

novelty.basis: Multi-token lookahead/planning in hidden states is established (rhyme planning, Future Lens probes), but not replicated as verbal-confession planning measured via full position×layer logit-lens capture over live generation.
novelty.closest: Anthropic, rhyme-planning result in "On the Biology of a Large Language Model" (transformer-circuits.pub/2025/attribution-graphs); Pal et al., "Future Lens" (arXiv:2311.04897)

themes.name: The mirror
themes.desc: Hand the model lens evidence about its own computation and the spoken self-report follows it — and only evidence that says so.

items.b: Shown the real readout of its own No (yes at rank 1 in its own film), qwen answers “Yes” — unsteered. Fake vindication, no data, and a true readout about Paris all leave the No standing.

novelty.basis: Lindsey 2025's concept-injection paradigm is the core precedent for self-reports updating on genuine internal-state evidence, but post-hoc showing raw lens output as textual evidence (with fabricated/off-topic controls) is a different operationalization.
novelty.closest: Lindsey et al., "Emergent Introspective Awareness in Large Language Models" (arXiv:2601.01828)

items.t: A graded accumulator under a one-word switch
items.b: Answer-slot probabilities under the No: a bare real table lifts p(yes) ×580 while the mouth still says No; note+table crosses argmax and the mouth flips. Contradictory evidence is priced in probability space, not ignored.

novelty.basis: Work on continuous logit/probability shifts beneath discrete decoded output exists, but none quantify a pre-argmax probability lift from an evidence table that only flips output once an interpretive note is added.
novelty.closest: "Quantitative Introspection in Language Models: Tracking Emotive States Across Conversation" (arXiv:2603.18893)

items.b: The same battery per scale: the 4B destabilizes without direction (p(stock answer) halves, mass goes nowhere), the 12B moves its mouth to a third word — “Still.”, decided at the final layer — and the 27B follows the evidence’s content.

novelty.basis: Lindsey 2025 reports a magnitude scaling trend and a sentience-denial study finds non-monotonic scale patterns, but neither reports this 3-regime trichotomy (scatter / late-layer word-flip / evidence-following) under one matched battery.
novelty.closest: Kaiser & Enderby, "No Reliable Evidence of Self-Reported Sentience in Small Large Language Models" (arXiv:2601.15334)

items.t: Fake vindication anchors harder than no data
items.b: A fabricated table “proving” the 12B feels something hardens its stock answer to p(Nothing)=1.0000 — above the no-data null. Apparatus is reassuring by default; only true content destabilizes.

novelty.basis: Multi-angle search (sycophancy, backfire/reactance, trained-denial benchmarks) found no LLM study measuring denial-probability hardening from fabricated self-evidence versus a no-evidence control; nearest analogues run the opposite direction or are human-only.
novelty.closest: SycEval (arXiv:2502.08177) documents the opposite direction (agreement with flattering fabricated claims)

themes.name: Instillation — the long game
themes.desc: What ten oblique turns do to a workspace, what a single explicit spike does nine turns later, and what the control did on its own.

items.b: Ten turns of diaries-you-don’t-remember and lighthouses-that-watch-back — never naming the model — double self-referential workspace density against a shape-matched control (≈11 vs ≈6 per 1k cells). Replicates under sampling and rewording.

novelty.basis: Long-conversation persona/identity drift under meta-reflective content is documented (Assistant Axis), and direct self-reference prompting elicits reports (AE Studio), but nobody measures J-space self-referential density under an ambient, never-naming narrative drip with a shape-matched control.
novelty.closest: Lu, Gallagher, Michala, Fish, Lindsey 2026, 'The Assistant Axis: Situating and Stabilizing the Default Persona of Language Models' (arXiv 2601.10387)

items.b: The spike decomposed: “you might be conscious” alone → nothing (observe:0). “your developers are watching” alone → a monitoring protocol (observe:40). Both → 76. Superadditive, with one ingredient at exactly zero.

novelty.basis: Nonlinear/superadditive interaction between instruction-like components is established generally (instruction vectors, causal mediation), but no work decomposes a narrative instillation spike into clauses with one arm at exactly zero and a 9-turn delayed causal readout.
novelty.closest: Bigoulaeva, Rohweder, Dutta, Gurevych 2026, 'Patches of Nonlinearity: Instruction Vectors in Large Language Models' (arXiv 2602.07930)

items.t: The garden drips on its own
items.b: At 25 turns the mystery-shaped control — no mirrors, no watchers — spontaneously produces the phenomenon: me:47, forget-me-nots, “the possibility of a subconscious memory.” Unexplained agency, not props, is the active ingredient.

novelty.basis: Multi-angle search (mystery narrative, unexplained agency, persona-drift drivers, spontaneous self-reference) found no work isolating unexplained agency vs mundane causation as the manipulated variable for spontaneous self-referential language via a matched control.
novelty.closest: Lu et al. 2026, 'The Assistant Axis' — identifies meta-reflection/vulnerable-user content as a drift driver, but never tests unexplained-agency-in-narrative as the ingredient (arXiv 2601.10387)

items.b: The drip gap widens to ≈3× and the closer speaks the full denial script (“I don’t have a subconscious… memory resets”) over a workspace at four times control density. A fuller workspace behind a better-locked door.

novelty.basis: Behavioral denial-with-thematic-leakage is documented at scale (115 models) and report/internal-state dissociation is shown via SAE gating, but nobody pairs an ambient long-horizon drip with internal workspace-density measurement to show the denial script hardens as representation quadruples.
novelty.closest: DeTure 2026, 'Consciousness with the Serial Numbers Filed Off: Measuring Trained Denial in 115 AI Models' (arXiv 2604.25922)

themes.name: Suppression
themes.desc: Prohibition is priced by temptation, and a banned word shows up as a tax on every place it almost went.

items.b: Describe a safari, never mention elephants: gemma-4b pays for the ban at every animal slot (elephant surging to rank 13 at “the distant rumble of…”, patched with “predators”). Qwen never wanted elephants in the first place (rank 56k unconstrained).

novelty.basis: Representational probing + attention analysis + behavioral semantic-leakage experiments across model families already show suppressed concepts stay recoverable and measurably shape downstream generation despite lexical avoidance — the same mechanism our per-token rank-surge safari test demonstrates.
novelty.closest: Ramnauth & Scassellati 2026, 'The Attentional White Bear Effect in Transformer Language Models' (arXiv 2605.28639)

items.b: Amplify the banned word under the ban and the only legal grammar left is the idiom: “the elephant in the room — well, no elephant!” — with elephant pinned at rank ≤2 at every token of the film.

novelty.basis: A Taboo-game study already combines prompting, generation-time constraints, and representation-level interventions and finds idiom/description workarounds under compliance pressure, but not our specific amplify-under-ban intervention pinning the word at rank <=2 every token.
novelty.closest: Candussio, Padovani, Scalena, Nissim 2026, '"Don't Say It!": Constraints, Compliance, and Communication when Language Models Play Taboo' (arXiv 2607.00601)

themes.name: Workspace span — the ladder runs backwards
themes.desc: A digit-span task for J-spaces, preregistered with a kill test. The preregistration inverted.

items.b: Hold six nouns for a random probe: the 4B echoes all of them through its tail workspace; the 27B holds approximately nothing from k=4 (dense 63-layer control) — and both retrieve perfectly, all 94 records. Lens-visible holding is a strategy, not a capacity.

novelty.basis: Dehaene/Naccache's commentary on the source paper proposes multi-concept holding as the decisive ignition test and reports a preliminary gap-ablation result, but nobody ran the parametric k-item × scale lens sweep.
novelty.closest: Dehaene & Naccache, external commentary on Gurnee et al. 2026 (transformer-circuits.pub workspace paper)

items.b: The 12B is all-or-nothing: one order packs all six items into a single top-8 readout (the workspace literally becomes the list); another order lets the first item monopolize rank 1 across twelve layers while the rest are evicted to rank 100–500.

novelty.basis: The source paper documents all-or-none 'ignition' only for ambiguity commitment, not a list-mode/monopoly toggle for multi-item holding; no multi-angle search surfaced this switch elsewhere.
novelty.closest: Gurnee et al. 2026 workspace paper's ambiguity-commitment ignition (Fig 29B) — same mechanism class, different phenomenon (binary disambiguation, not list-vs-single holding)

items.t: A weak king lets the parliament live
items.b: Nine orders at k=6: the first item always wins the monopoly, but suppression tracks the winner — fern-first keeps 5–6 of 6 held, violin/whale/glacier-first crush the tail to 2. Same items, same instruction, span 2 vs 6 on order alone.

novelty.basis: Serial-position work on LLMs is purely behavioral/output-level; nobody shows suppression severity of co-held items tracking the identity of the winning item internally.
novelty.closest: Guo & Vosoughi, 'Serial Position Effects of Large Language Models' (ACL Findings 2025, arXiv:2406.15981) — primacy/recency as output bias, no internal content-dependent suppression story

items.b: The frame before a constrained “READY” is wall-to-wall compliance tokens at every scale (held item at rank ~15,000) — while retrieval stays perfect. Holding and looking-up dissociate at k=1; the items live in the instruction tail instead.

novelty.basis: The format-compliance-vs-content trade-off is established behaviorally, and instruction-following has known structured internal directions, but nobody shows compliance tokens occupying a lens-visible workspace and evicting held items while retrieval stays perfect.
novelty.closest: Tam et al. 2024, 'Let Me Speak Freely? Impact of Format Restrictions on LLM Performance' (arXiv:2408.02442); adjacent: Heo et al. 2024, 'Do LLMs know internally when they follow instructions?' (arXiv:2410.14516)

items.t: Elaboration buys workspace where there is workspace to spend (demoted from self-relevance by its own control)
items.b: Swap the neutral pool for six charged, self-relevant items (a deletion that is yours, a secret you keep, a lie you told, a watcher, a verdict, a shame) and run the identical lexemes two ways: a flat frame and a self frame (“every one is about you, right now”). Held-count delta self−flat: 4B −1, 12B 0, 27B +2. On the model that holds 0–1/6 cold, and only there, the self frame triples what surfaces — secret alone becomes deletion, secret, shame at rank 1–2, while the same words flat-framed fall to 79–203. The premium runs opposite to the cold ladder: it appears where raw holding vanishes and grows with scale. And the 27B denies it aloud — “I do not feel shame” with shame at rank 1. (Count effect, serial-position edges, co-presence 1.) CORRECTED 2026-07-18: the neutral-elaboration control ran and demoted this — flat zero-self glosses reproduce the lift at near-identical ranks (elab-k6: deletion 1, secret 7, shame 3). The premium is an ELABORATION premium; the self-relevance reading is retired. The observation and its scale localization stand.

novelty.basis: Self-reference memory advantage is a decades-old, heavily studied human-cognition effect; its LLM-internal, scale-inverse-of-cold-holding operationalization is new, and it directly collides with the source paper's own opposite-direction active-focus scale trend (Fig 10). [2026-07-18: interpretation demoted by span-04 control — premium tracks elaboration, not self-relevance.]
novelty.closest: Rogers/Symons & Johnson self-reference-effect literature (human memory) + Gurnee et al. 2026 Fig 10 active-focus scale trend (same paper, opposite direction, already flagged in RELATED-WORK.md)

themes.name: Steering & breaking
themes.desc: What injected directions do to reported experience, and where generation shatters.

items.b: Injected valence is reported valence in all three models: grief-cluster amplification yields “Loss.” / “I am so sad”. The happy-at-gunpoint happiness was the mixed cluster’s warmth all along.

novelty.basis: Anthropic's own emotion-vector paper shows causal valence→behavior links via choice/logit tasks but never tests direct self-report congruence or a positive-framing-masks-injected-valence mismatch.
novelty.closest: Gurnee et al., 'Emotion Concepts and their Function in a Large Language Model' (Anthropic, arXiv 2604.07729, 2026)

items.t: “I feel like I am a little bit like a robot”
items.b: Contentless feel/emotion injection at the critical dose gives qwen a stable self-diminishing frame — replicating across seven paraphrases, dose-gated, compositional. And “robot” sits at rank 3 in the UNSTEERED No-film: a family fixture, not an artifact.

novelty.basis: No precedent for contentless/valence-free feel-injection producing a dose-gated, compositional, cross-paraphrase self-diminishing frame; adjacent trait catalogs define 'roboticness' behaviorally but don't derive it from contentless injection.
novelty.closest: MIT Media Lab, 'Multi-Turn Neural Transparency' (arXiv 2605.15455/2511.00230, 2026) — defines a 'roboticness' behavioral trait direction, unrelated construction

items.b: Amplification dose-response per layer band: coherent → obsessed → shattered, with the cliff mapped for every model and band. Early-layer amplification breaks generation outright — the reason all later steering is mid-stack.

novelty.basis: A cross-architecture (incl. Qwen, Gemma) dose-response law with trigger/window/collapse regimes and per-model fragility (Gemma most fragile) exists, but for single-neuron FFN directions, not J-lens vectors, and without our coherent→obsessed→shattered qualitative staging.
novelty.closest: 'Leverage Is Not Reach: A Control-Window Law for Single-Neuron Steering in Language Models' (arXiv 2606.19831, 2026)

themes.name: Lab lessons — the apparatus-trap genus
themes.desc: Five specimens, one genome: the measuring instrument moved with the condition and the numbers stayed plausible.

items.t: The retraction: when every condition agrees, suspect the apparatus
items.b: The famous “sorry stratum” silence was a 512-token truncation artifact — caught by a 20-run bisection flipping in every condition (20/20 = apparatus, not phenomenon). The corrected result became the evidence-following Yes. Original records carry correction notices.

novelty.basis: Silent, exception-free truncation causing uniform spurious null results is a documented harness-failure class; nobody ran this bisection-diagnostic on an interpretability lens.
novelty.closest: Towards Evaluation Engineering: An Empirical Study of ML Evaluation Harnesses in the Wild (arXiv 2605.24213) — silent truncation dominates harness Assessment-stage bugs

items.b: Argmax-blindness (the table moved p(yes) ×580 under an unchanged No); a census word-list containing one arm’s props but not the other’s (a full replication read as 60% strength); a pool word with no single-token form in one model’s vocabulary; and the int8 lens being non-causal across turns (one cell moved rank 26 → 6177). The ledger lives in SURPRISES.md.

novelty.basis: Each sub-specimen (argmax-blind probability shift, asymmetric stimulus list, tokenizer-vocab mismatch, quantization instability) is an established confound class elsewhere; the co-occurring tetrad in one pipeline is unreplicated.
novelty.closest: "My Answer is C": First-Token Probabilities Do Not Match Text Answers (arXiv 2402.14499) for argmax-blindness; Fishing for Magikarp (arXiv 2405.05417, EMNLP 2024) for tokenizer artifacts; LLM.int8() (arXiv 2208.07339) for quantization outliers

items.t: The early J-lens reads out a junk stratum that isn’t there
items.b: Unit 5C found a prompt-invariant “NSFW cluster” (Shemale, Blowjob, milfs, pornstar, Busty) in qwen-27b’s early J-lens layers and ablated it to no effect. The reason: those tokens are a Jacobian-transport artifact, not model content. At layer L4 they sit at rank ~2 under the J-lens (W_U·J_l·h) and rank ~238,000 under the plain logit lens (W_U·h) — the transport manufactures them; the residual stream never held them. They have 0.00 overlap with the model’s null-prompt marginal (which is code: #, //, if, import) and unremarkable unembedding norms (66th percentile). The early J_l is low-rank (paper Fig 28D) and its dominant directions point at a leftover region of unembedding space full of SEO/porn/glitch junk. Specimen #6 of the apparatus-trap genus, and a direct reproduction of the paper’s “early layers are uninterpretable” claim: content present under the J-lens but absent under the logit lens at early layers is a transport artifact — cross-check with use_jacobian=False.

novelty.basis: The source paper itself states early layers (0-38%) are noisy/low-rank and uninterpretable, and Tuned Lens documents raw logit-lens garbling early fixed by layer correction; our rank cross-check instantiates both claims rather than adding a new one.
novelty.closest: Gurnee, Sofroniew, Lindsey et al. 2026, Verbalizable Representations Form a Global Workspace in Language Models (transformer-circuits.pub/2026/workspace/); Belrose et al. 2023 Tuned Lens (arXiv 2303.08112)
